name: Southeast Asian Straits

name: Port of St. Petersburg

name: Strait of Hormuz Route
description: The world's most important oil chokepoint. Approximately 20% of global petroleum passes through this narrow strait between Iran and Oman/UAE daily.

name: Strait of Malacca Route
description: The narrowest and busiest shipping chokepoint in the world. Connects the Indian Ocean to the South China Sea, carrying ~25% of all maritime trade including 80% of China's oil imports.

name: Suez Canal Route (Europe–Asia)
description: The world's most critical maritime chokepoint. Connects the Mediterranean to the Red Sea, enabling Europe–Asia trade without circumnavigating Africa. Handles ~12% of global trade and ~30% of container traffic.
